data.title: Test hygiene: make pytest independent of a running Postgres (suite blocks ~6min on DB connect timeouts when container is down)

data.body: Discovered during NAVI-13 (2026-06-13). Root cause: 7 test files (test_health/ask/cli/runs_list/sap_review/static_ui/trace) drive the API via TestClient, which calls check_db against the real DATABASE_URL (Postgres 127.0.0.1:5432). When the container is down (e.g. after stop.bat), each hits psycopg connection-timeout retries -> full 'uv run pytest' takes ~392s vs ~2.5s with Postgres up. Tests pass either way (136 passed/0 failed) but the wall-clock blowup makes gate checks look hung. Fix options for planning: (a) the session/conftest fixture already provides in-memory SQLite -- route check_db / the API DB dependency through it in tests so no real DB is needed; (b) mock/skip the DB-health path under test; (c) mark DB-integration tests with a marker and a fast default. Goal: 'uv run pytest' is fast and deterministic regardless of whether Postgres/Docker is running. backlog.

REVIEWED (cold) commit 08078c5 on dev: diff (db.py, api.py, test_health.py, test_static_ui.py) + final state of check_db/engine/health + grep of all tests for TestClient/health/get_session. Toolchain with Postgres DOWN: ruff 'All checks passed!'; mypy 'Success: no issues found in 36 source files'; pytest 143 passed / 1 skipped in 2.36s (was ~392s with DB down — the fix works, suite no longer blocks on connect).

Per-criterion: (1) PASS — check_db(session: Session) -> bool runs session.execute(text('SELECT 1')), keeps try/except->False contract; no prod path other than the get_session dependency builds the probe session. (2) PASS — /health depends on get_session, passes session to check_db, always 200, db ok/error. (3) PASS — engine has connect_args={'connect_timeout': _DB_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_S=5}, correct libpq/psycopg v3 param in seconds, positive value, up-Postgres connect unaffected (prior up run 2.47s). (4) PASS — test_health overrides get_session with in-memory SQLite (StaticPool); test_check_db_false_on_error uses a MagicMock whose execute raises RuntimeError, cast(Session, broken) keeps mypy clean, genuinely exercises the except path. (5) PASS — both /health-calling static_ui tests call _use_sqlite(); missing-dir no-op test correctly skips it; all overrides cleared in finally. (6) PASS — ruff+mypy clean, suite green and fast.

Override-leak audit: test_health._client() clears in finally (generator's finally runs as loop exhausts); both test_static_ui tests clear app.dependency_overrides in finally. No leak from this commit. Prod /health reasoning confirmed: get_session does lazy 'with Session(engine)' (no connect), check_db -> session.execute -> connect fails within 5s timeout -> caught -> False -> db:'error', no exception escapes (context exit on never-connected session does not raise). Prod still reports db:'ok' when Postgres up.

Minor note (NOT a finding, out of scope): test_cli.py's _client_for returns TestClient(app) and sets a get_session override without a try/finally clear — a pre-existing pattern untouched by this commit; it only ever installs a SQLite override (never leaks an un-overridden real-DB state), so it cannot hide real-DB regressions. Leave as-is for NAVI-21; could be tidied later.

VERDICT: PASS
